sentence: Usted sabe que el zorro azul corre por el campo.
ipa: usteð saβe ke el soro asul kore poɾ el kampo
gloss_en: You know the blue fox runs across the field.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. Equatoguinean formal address (usted) per hualde2005.

sentence: Tome la taza de agua y venga al mercado.
ipa: tome la tasa de aɡa i benɡa al meɾkaðo
gloss_en: Take the cup of water and come to the market.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. usted imperatives (tome, venga) per hualde2005.

sentence: El niño tiene un perro grande y manso.
ipa: el niɲo tjene un pero ɡɾande i manso
gloss_en: The child has a big, tame dog.
notes: palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general Equatoguinean usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Mañana el joven juega en la plaza con usted.
ipa: maɲana el xoben xweɣa en la plasa kon usteð
gloss_en: Tomorrow the young man plays in the square with you.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. formal address (con usted) per hualde2005.

sentence: La calle está llena de gente joven.
ipa: la kaʝe esta ʝena de xente xoben
gloss_en: The street is full of young people.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: ¿Quiere usted arroz con pollo o prefiere pescado?
ipa: kjeɾe usteð aros kon poʝo o pɾefjeɾe peskaðo
gloss_en: Do you want rice with chicken or do you prefer fish?
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. formal interrogative (quiere, prefiere) per hualde2005.

sentence: Busque unos zapatos nuevos en aquel mercado.
ipa: buske unos sapatos nwebos en akel meɾkaðo
gloss_en: Look for some new shoes in that market.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. usted imperative (busque) per hualde2005.

sentence: La lluvia cae fuerte sobre el tejado viejo.
ipa: la ʝubja kae fweɾte sobɾe el texaðo bjexo
gloss_en: The rain falls hard on the old roof.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Nosotros queremos una casa buena y barata.
ipa: nosotɾos keɾemos una kasa bwena i baɾata
gloss_en: We want a good, cheap house.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: El carro rojo corre rápido por la carretera.
ipa: el karo roxo kore rapiðo poɾ la kareteɾa
gloss_en: The red car runs fast along the highway.
notes: strong-R trill [r] per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Ese señor tiene mucha tierra en la montaña.
ipa: ese seɲoɾ tjene mutʃa tjera en la montaɲa
gloss_en: That gentleman has a lot of land on the mountain.
notes: palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: No vaya solo, ande con su compañero.
ipa: no baʝa solo ande kon su kompaɲeɾo
gloss_en: Don't go alone, go with your companion.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. usted negative imperative (vaya, ande) per hualde2005.

sentence: La manzana y la naranja están en la mesa.
ipa: la mansana i la naɾanxa estan en la mesa
gloss_en: The apple and the orange are on the table.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: ¿Vive usted lejos del parque nuevo?
ipa: bibe usteð lexos del paɾke nwebo
gloss_en: Do you live far from the new park?
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. formal address (vive usted) per hualde2005.

sentence: El caballo blanco pasta junto al arroyo.
ipa: el kaβaʝo blanko pasta xunto al aroʝo
gloss_en: The white horse grazes next to the stream.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; strong-R trill [r]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Compre pan y guarde un poco para luego.
ipa: kompɾe pan i ɡaɾde un poko paɾa lweɣo
gloss_en: Buy bread and save some for later.
notes: tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. usted imperatives (compre, guarde) per hualde2005.

sentence: Los zapatos viejos ya no sirven para nada.
ipa: los sapatos bjexos ʝa no siɾben paɾa naða
gloss_en: The old shoes are no good for anything anymore.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Aquella niña baila con mucha alegría.
ipa: akeʝa niɲa bajla kon mutʃa aleɡɾia
gloss_en: That girl dances with great joy.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; palatal nasal ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.

sentence: Tiene que llamar a su madre temprano.
ipa: tjene ke ʝamaɾ a su madɾe tempɾano
gloss_en: You have to call your mother early.
notes: yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; tonic diphthong ⟨ie/ue⟩ [je we] per quilis1993. formal address (tiene) per hualde2005.

sentence: El sol brilla sobre el mar azul y tranquilo.
ipa: el sol bɾiʝa sobɾe el maɾ asul i tɾankilo
gloss_en: The sun shines over the blue, calm sea.
notes: seseo ⟨c/z⟩ merged to [s]; yeísmo ⟨ll/y⟩ to [ʝ]; cross-word sandhi junction per quilis1993. general usage per hualde2005.
